metadata.title:
This is the age of the woman.
And the future also is hers.

pages.raw_text:
IL


1t is less than so years since American girls were re-
fused admission to the colleges their brothers attended
Now there are more in the colleges than men.


Almost every agency Of modern times caters to woman
Merchants vie for her custom The pulpit wants her
strong support The press seeks her favor and patronage
Woman is longer behind the throne She is the throne


All this change of few years seems radical depar
ture. But cast your eyes Europe.


The of Britain pounding at the doors OF
parliament seeking suffrage.


In Germany the gates of the ancient universities have
been lifted from their hinges to let women in.


In Italy the parliament has appointed commission
to arrange if possible, for equal suffrage.


The French have gone ahead OF men IN the
field of scientific discovery And in England of the II
best selling books every written by woman.


Even in darkest Russia physicians are teach
ing the doctors of the world advance methods in hospital
work.


But most striking of all, in Finland-tlink of tin
Tnland!-19 women occupy seats as law makers III
the legislature of that duchy!


In point of fact. in its appreciation and advance of
women-great as that advance has beenAmerica is in
some respects far behind


This is the woman's age.


In this equation of modern life is man to be the
N-the unknown quantity?
